返回本批次

芝加哥艺术家为城市社区创作旅游海报

Chicago artist creates tourism posters for city's neighborhoods

www.chicagotribune.com 作者 NaOH 分数 77 评论 34 2026/03/27 07:18
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 如果您曾经见过一张 20 世纪 30 年代应公共工程振兴管理局 (Works Progress Administration) 的要求制作的宣传美国国家公园的海报，那么您就会对插画家兼平面设计师 Steve Shanabruch 为芝加哥的一些社区和地标创作的旅游风格海报有所了解。它们具有大胆的线条和鲜艳的色彩，视觉上引人注目。恩格尔伍德的海报以黄砖住宅耶鲁大楼阳光明媚的中庭为特色。诺伍德公园的海报以 Superdawg 汽车热狗摊独特、色彩缤纷的外观为特色。他的最新作品是柳条公园的海报，上面有一辆蓝线“L”火车。沙纳布鲁赫与任何城市机构没有任何隶属关系。他绘制这些海报的动力是自我驱动的，这些海报可以在他的网站上出售。该项目于 15 年前开始，其概念更为简单：为芝加哥社区创建徽标。
  2. “我讨厌我的工作，”沙纳布鲁赫说。 “我是一家销售公司的设计师，我并没有受到创意方面的挑战。所以这只是一个满足我创意需求的个人项目。我在 2011 年开始设计邻里和社区区域的标志。”沙纳布鲁赫来自芝加哥。他在贝弗利长大。 “芝加哥和芝加哥的历史根深蒂固在我的血液中。我的父亲为这座城市创立了芝加哥平房协会，并从其起步阶段开始运营。所以这座城市的特质一直吸引着我。我想这对于任何设计师或创意人士来说可能都是如此。”艺术家 Steve Shanabruch 为其芝加哥社区项目创作的柳条公园海报。 (Steve Shanabruch)1 of 8 艺术家 Steve Shanabruch 为其芝加哥社区项目创作的柳条公园海报。 (Steve Shanabruch)Expand 他首先每周创建三个徽标。 “在生孩子之前，我经常去流浪，”他谈到自己的灵感时说道。这些标志“基于对这些社区的个人经历”和具有代表性的图像。洪堡公园的标志是分区街上的波多黎各国旗拱门。海德公园的标志采用了弗兰克·劳埃德·赖特 (Frank Lloyd Wright) 的罗比故居 (Robie House) 的彩色玻璃。建伍标志采用 Powhatan 公寓楼的装饰艺术图案。新城标志（包括后院的区域）以一头猪为特色，参考了该社区作为屠宰场的历史。南芝加哥的标志是一辆看起来像是在坡道上加速的汽车，这是参考了电影《蓝调兄弟》中蓝调汽车跳过部分凸起的第 95 街大桥时的场景。沙纳布鲁赫说，人们有时会主动寻求使用这些标志。 “实际上，我当时只是开车经过鲍曼维尔，在横幅上看到了它，我当时就想，天哪，我忘了我说过他们可以做到这一点！”大约一年后，他对标志感到厌倦。这就是促使他进行社区海报项目的下一次迭代的原因。他拥有一张 WPA 国家公园的旧海报，有一天看着它，他想：如果我专注于芝加哥的各个社区，那会很有趣。 “仅仅根据徽标的制作，就可以明显看出人们对自己的社区非常自豪，”他说。 “这是一种很好的方式来展示那些从未被展示过的社区。芝加哥有很多艺术作品，但其中很多都是基于市中心或湖滨或任何时尚社区。但是北朗代尔的人？他们可能很高兴住在那里，并且很高兴看到它出现在海报上。我自 2012 年以来一直在出售这些海报，所以我知道某个社区的海报是否会卖不出去。但我仍然会这样做，因为，它具有创造性的满足感，第二，让人们看到每个社区都很重要，即使它被媒体抛弃了，而且我想创造出颂扬这座城市的艺术。”如今，沙纳布鲁赫住在波蒂奇公园。波蒂奇剧院在海报中占据了显着位置。这感觉很直观。有些海报更违反直觉。例如，罗杰斯公园在洛约拉大学校园内设有麦当娜德拉斯特拉达教堂。 “我不信教，我与洛约拉没有任何关系。但我这样做主要是为了实现创造性，我喜欢装饰艺术，想画那个装饰艺术教堂。但我也了解到，我的教堂海报卖得不好。”最初只是一个非正式的副业项目，现在已占沙纳布鲁赫收入的一半。除了他的网站之外，他的艺术品还在城镇各处出售，包括威利斯大厦观景台礼品店。他一年多前辞去了工作，现在也以自由职业者的身份与客户合作。当芝加哥​​保护组织上周发布濒危遗址名单时，沙纳布鲁赫受雇绘制一张海报，海报上有芝加哥证券交易所交易室楼层的一个专栏，该楼层目前位于艺术学院。他估计到目前为止他已经调查了大约 60 个社区。 “我从来没有计划过。我只是一时兴起，”他说。 “但我真的很想做欧文公园，因为我收到了大量的要求。但通常是因为我开车经过一个社区，我会说，‘哦，天哪，我已经很长时间没来过这里了，’然后我会开始注意到一些事情，通常这会激发我的灵感。这绝对是偶然的。”然后他在 iPad 上绘制海报。有几年，他出版了 10 或 11 幅印刷品。有时只有五六个，“取决于我的工作量和生活中发生的事情。” Shanabruch 还为该项目添加了第三个元素：基于城镇周围标牌的字体。最新的灵感来自查塔姆附近标志性的 Pride Cleaners 大楼，该大楼最近宣布即将关闭。所有字体都可以免费下载。 2026 年 3 月 9 日，艺术家 Steve Shanabruch 在他芝加哥的家中创作一件新作品。（Brian Cassella/芝加哥论坛报）“从霓虹灯标志到雕刻在建筑物上的字母“芝加哥的立面有着令人惊叹的标牌的悠久历史，”他在自己的网站上说道。“这些年来，许多标牌已经丢失，但我找到了一种方法来保存我们仍然拥有的东西，并通过不断扩大的标牌灵感字体名册来纪念我们失去的东西。”他将其中一种字体称为 Comiskey，该字体是基于一张棒球场的照片，他猜测这张照片来自 20 世纪 60 年代或 70 年代。这些字母上有马刺，使字体看起来几乎是西部乡村的。 “当时的白袜队很古怪。”还有一个名为 Tap Room，以湖景区林肯 Tap Room 的标志为蓝本。 “我不是受过训练的印刷师，但我做的第一个印刷是 Starsiak，它位于密尔沃基和分区的 Starsiak Clothing 大楼上，我只是想重新创建它。所以我制作了所有26个字母和10个数字和标点符号，然后制作了一个字体文件。我当时想，嗯，这是一个有趣的挑战。所以我就开始制作它们，然后人们开始给我发照片说，‘嘿，你应该用这个制作一种字体。’其中一些是标志性标志，有些则不是。”他说，当他应该专注于其他事情时，他发现自己正在研究字体。 “我称之为拖延工作。我很有效率，但不是在我应该高效的事情上。”这些字体可以免费下载。 “我不在乎人们如何使用它们，只要他们不将它们用于特朗普广告或 ICE 的东西即可。我想我无法控制这一点，但这是我的偏好。前几天我在 Edgewater Candles，他们给了我一件他们的衬衫，因为上面有我的字体。太棒了。然后我看到有人使用我的一种字体来制作专辑插图，我想，嘿，这很酷。所以看到它很有趣——这就是为什么我把它们免费放在那里。我内心深处自卑的设计师就像是，没有人会为这些付钱，所以我不妨把它们拿出来。我创造了它们，为什么不让人们使用它们呢？”相比之下，Shanabruch 网站上的印刷品并不是免费的。但价格——每张海报 28 美元——并不离谱。 “就是这样，我希望海报能够被人们所看到，”他说。 “这很好，因为有些人会离开芝加哥，他们会买五六个来代表他们曾经居住过的所有社区，这样他们仍然可以拥有那样的记忆。 “我的意思是，每当有人说他们喜欢我的艺术时，这都是令人兴奋的。显然，人们会购买它，但人们喜欢我制作的东西仍然让我感到惊讶。”

Original Summary

  1. If you’ve ever seen a poster from the 1930s promoting one of America’s national parks, created at the behest of the Works Progress Administration, then you have a good idea of the kind of tourism-style posters the illustrator and graphic designer Steve Shanabruch has created for a number of Chicago neighborhoods and landmarks. They are visually striking, with bold lines and vivid colors. The poster for Englewood features the sunny atrium of the yellow brick residential Yale Building. The poster for Norwood Park features the distinctive, colorful exterior of the Superdawg drive-in hot dog stand. His latest is a poster for Wicker Park featuring a Blue Line “L” train. Shanabruch has no affiliation with any city agency. His impetus to draw these posters — which are available for sale on his website — is self-driven. The project began 15 years ago with a simpler concept: Creating logos for Chicago neighborhoods.
  2. “I hated my job,” says Shanabruch. “I was a designer with a sales company and I just wasn’t being challenged creatively. So it was just a personal project to fulfill my creative needs. I started the logos of neighborhoods and community areas in 2011.” Shanabruch is originally from Chicago. He grew up in Beverly. “Chicago and Chicago history is ingrained in my blood. My father started the Chicago Bungalow Association for the city and ran it from its infancy. So the city’s idiosyncrasies have always appealed to me. I think that’s probably true for any designer or creative person.” A poster of Wicker Park created by artist Steve Shanabruch for his Chicago Neighborhoods project. (Steve Shanabruch)1 of 8A poster of Wicker Park created by artist Steve Shanabruch for his Chicago Neighborhoods project. (Steve Shanabruch)Expand He began by creating three logos a week. “I used to wander a lot more before I had a child,” he says of his inspirations. The logos were “based on personal experience with those neighborhoods” and imagery that felt representative. The Humboldt Park logo features the Puerto Rican flag arch on Division Street. The Hyde Park logo features the stained glass from Frank Lloyd Wright’s Robie House. The Kenwood logo has an Art Deco graphic from the Powhatan Apartment building. The New City logo (an area that encompasses Back of the Yards) features a pig, referencing the neighborhood’s history as the site of slaughterhouses. The logo for South Chicago features a car that looks like it’s speeding up a ramp, which is a reference to “The Blues Brothers” movie when the Bluesmobile jumps the partially raised 95th Street Bridge. Occasionally, Shanabruch says, people would reach out about using the logos. “I was actually just driving through Bowmanville and saw it on a banner and I was like, oh man, I forgot that I said they could do that!” After a year or so, he burned out on logos. That is what led him to the next iteration of the project with neighborhood posters. He owns a copy of one of those old WPA National Parks posters, and looking at it one day, he thought: That could be fun if I focus on various Chicago neighborhoods. “Just based on doing the logos, it became obvious that people have so much pride in their neighborhoods,” he says. “And it’s a nice way to feature neighborhoods that don’t ever get featured. There’s a lot of Chicago art out there, but so much of it is based on downtown or the lakefront or any of the hip neighborhoods. But somebody in North Lawndale? They’re probably happy they live there and would appreciate seeing it represented on a poster. I’ve been selling these since 2012, so I know if a certain neighborhood poster is not going to sell. But I’ll still do it because one, it’s creatively fulfilling, and two, it’s really important for people to be seen. Every neighborhood is important, even if it gets dumped on by the media. And I want to create art that celebrates the city.” These days, Shanabruch lives in Portage Park. And the Portage Theater is featured prominently in the posters. That feels intuitive. Some of the posters are more counterintuitive. Rogers Park, for example, features the Madonna Della Strada chapel on the campus of Loyola University. “I’m not religious, I have no ties to Loyola. But I’m doing this mainly for creative fulfillment and I love Art Deco and wanted to draw that Art Deco church. But I’ve also learned that my posters featuring churches don’t sell well.” What began as an informal side project now makes up half of Shanabruch’s income. In addition to his website, his art is sold around town, including the Willis Tower Skydeck gift shop. He quit his job more than a year ago and now also works with clients on a freelance basis. When Preservation Chicago released its list of endangered sites last week, Shanabruch was hired to draw a poster featuring a column from the Chicago Stock Exchange trading room floor, currently housed at the Art Institute. He estimates that he’s done around 60 neighborhoods so far. “I never plan it out. I do it on a whim,” he says. “But I really want to do Irving Park, because I get a ton of requests for that. But usually it’s because I’m driving through a neighborhood and I’ll be like, ‘Oh, man, I haven’t been here in a long time,’ and then I’ll start to notice things and usually that inspires me. It’s definitely serendipitous.” He then draws the posters on an iPad. Some years, he’s released 10 or 11 prints. Sometimes it’s just five or six, “depending on my workload and what’s going on in my life.” Shanabruch has also added a third element to the project: Fonts based on signage around town. The newest is inspired by the iconic Pride Cleaners building in the Chatham neighborhood, which recently announced that it is closing. All the fonts can be downloaded for free. Artist Steve Shanabruch works on a new piece at his Chicago home on March 9, 2026. (Brian Cassella/Chicago Tribune) “From neon signs to letters sculpted into a building’s facade, Chicago has a long history of amazing signage,” he says on his website. “So many of these signs have been lost over the years, but I have found a way to preserve what we still have and commemorate what we have lost through an ever-expanding roster of signage-inspired fonts.” He calls one of the fonts Comiskey and it’s based on a photo of the ballpark stadium that he guesses is from the 1960s or ’70s. The letters have spurs on them, giving the font an almost country-western look. “The White Sox were quirky back then.” There’s another called Tap Room, based on the signage of the Lincoln Tap Room in Lakeview. “I’m not a trained typographer, but the first one I did was Starsiak, which was on the Starsiak Clothing building on Milwaukee and Division and I just wanted to recreate that. So I made all 26 letters and 10 numbers and punctuation and then made a font file. And I was like, well, that was a fun challenge. So I just started making them, and then people started sending me pictures saying, ‘Hey, you should make a font of this one.’ Some of them are iconic signs, some of them aren’t.” He says he finds himself working on fonts when he’s supposed to be focused on something else. “I call it procrasti-working. I’m being productive, just not on the thing I’m supposed to be productive on.” The fonts are free to download. “I don’t care how people use them, as long as they’re not using them for Trump ads or ICE stuff. I guess I can’t control that, but that’s my preference. I was in Edgewater Candles the other day and they gave me a shirt of theirs because it had one of my fonts on it. It was awesome. And then I saw someone used one of my fonts for album artwork and I thought, hey, that’s cool. So it’s fun to see — that’s why I put them out there for free. The low self-esteem designer in me is like, nobody would pay for these, so I might as well just get them out there. I made them, so why not let people use them?” By contrast, the prints on Shanabruch’s website are not free. But the price — $28 per poster — isn’t outlandish. “That’s the thing, I want the posters to be accessible,” he says. “It’s nice because some people will leave Chicago and they’ll buy five or six of them to represent all the neighborhoods they’ve lived in so they can still have that memory. “I mean, it’s exciting any time anyone says they like my art. Obviously, people buy it, but it’s still astounding to me that people like the stuff I make.”

评论（默认中文）

vogtb 2026/03/27 09:10
这让我想起另一位制作城市等距地图的艺术家。我买了一个，它挂在我办公室的墙上。 https://www.etsy.com/listing/1673296980/map-of-chicago-loop-...

有一个了解这座城市的人来指导总是很有趣的。我们将花 20 分钟指出我们曾经工作过的所有建筑、我们吃过饭的地方，或者《熊》或《逃亡者》的拍摄地。

This reminds me of another artist that did an isometric map of the city. I bought one and it hangs on my office wall. https://www.etsy.com/listing/1673296980/map-of-chicago-loop-...

Always fun to have someone over who knows the city. We'll spend 20 minutes pointing to all the buildings we've worked in, places we've eaten, or where The Bear or The Fugitive were filmed.

我把这个挂在我的客厅里，尽管大多数人似乎没有得到参考 https://wondercitystudio.com/collections/new-product-collect...
I have this hanging in my living room, though most people don't seem to get the reference https://wondercitystudio.com/collections/new-product-collect...

canpan 2026/03/27 09:59
很不错！在东京，我们有这幅壁画，其中一些部分是使用墙上的屏幕进行动画处理的。 https://www.teamlab.art/w/skytreemural/tokyoskytree/
Very nice! In Tokyo we had this mural some parts were animated using screens in the wall. https://www.teamlab.art/w/skytreemural/tokyoskytree/

mikeaskew4 2026/03/27 10:15
不要忘记回溯。
Don’t forget Backdraft.
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/03/27 07:59
不清楚为什么它今天会达到 HN，但这些在芝加哥足够流行，有点陈词滥调。不管海报多么有说服力，我想如果你计划去风景秀丽的盖尔伍德旅行，你会感到失望。
Not clear why this is hitting HN today, but these are popular enough in Chicago to be kind of a cliche. No matter how convincing the poster is, I think you'll be disappointed if you plan a trip to visit scenic Galewood.
在 HN 中查看
perardi 2026/03/27 10:01
当你搬到芝加哥时，购买其中一件完全是陈词滥调的成年礼。

当我看着挂在我桌子上方的埃奇沃特海报时，我正在打字。

It’s a total cliché rite of passage to buy one of these when you move to Chicago.

And I am typing that as I look at the Edgewater poster hanging right there over my desk.

在 HN 中查看
60654 2026/03/27 11:18
是的，这些海报已经非常出名了，让我们看看，也许有十年了？但了解更多关于这位艺术家的信息是很酷的。

而且，没有意识到他也发布了一堆很酷的字体： https://tomato-giraffe-jpm4.squarespace.com/fonts。

Yeah the posters have been pretty well known for, let's see, maybe a decade now? But it's cool to read more about the artist.

And also, didn't realize he released a bunch of cool fonts, too: https://tomato-giraffe-jpm4.squarespace.com/fonts.

prawn 2026/03/27 08:16
这个诱饵足以让我跳进谷歌地图，通过街景随机查看几条盖尔伍德街道。显然非常郊区，但看起来这里很适合散步，直到你厌倦了千篇一律的布局。休·海夫纳童年的家作为额外奖励。
This was bait enough that I jumped into Google Maps to look at a few random Galewood streets via street view. Obviously very suburban, but looks like it'd make for a nice stroll until you tired of the cookie-cutter layout. Hugh Hefner's childhood home as a bonus.
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/03/27 08:21
我住在它的街对面。没关系！它只是芝加哥最无聊的街区之一。不过，步行即可到达约翰尼牛肉店，这是该市最好的牛肉店。
I live across the street from it. It's fine! It's just one of the most boring neighborhoods in Chicago. Walking distance to Johnnie's Beef, though, which is the best beef spot in the city.

tptacek 2026/03/27 08:45
盖尔伍德本身？是的，不，尽管它位于芝加哥最著名的建筑区之一的街对面。但海报呢？它们是故意向 WPA 风格致敬。
Of Galewood itself? Yeah, no, though it's across the street from one of the most famous architectural areas in Chicagoland. But the posters? They're deliberately an homage to WPA style.

mikeaskew4 2026/03/27 10:12

South Shore Line ones, too…which may have also been WPA style. I should look that up…

3eb7988a1663 2026/03/27 08:25
对于一些不太严肃的芝加哥公报，有 SquirrelTruth[0]。一个 Kickstarter 的创建是为了在 CTA 中张贴有关松鼠危险的标志。我不知道到底有多少变体，但我唯一见过的就是“从统计上看，这列火车上至少有一个‘人’实际上是7只穿着人类服装的松鼠。不要成为受害者。”

[0] https://www.squirreltruth.com/

For some less serious Chicago bulletins, there was SquirrelTruth[0]. A kickstarter was created to post signs in the CTA about the danger of squirrels. I am not sure how many variations there were, but the only one I ever saw was, "Statistically speaking, at least one 'person' on this train is actually 7 squirrels wearing a human suit. Don't be a victim."

[0] https://www.squirreltruth.com/

stavros 2026/03/27 08:36
> “我的意思是，任何时候有人说他们喜欢我的艺术，这都是令人兴奋的。显然，人们会购买它，但人们喜欢我制作的东西仍然让我感到惊讶。”

我喜欢这个。

> “I mean, it’s exciting any time anyone says they like my art. Obviously, people buy it, but it’s still astounding to me that people like the stuff I make.”

I love this.

tptacek 2026/03/27 08:33
恭喜！我去了芝加哥（出生和长大）->旧金山->芝加哥->旧金山->芝加哥（绕道安娜堡）。

您要搬到芝加哥哪里？这真是一座奇妙的城市。

Congratulations! I did Chicago (born and raised) -> SF -> Chicago -> SF -> Chicago (detouring through Ann Arbor).

Where in Chicago are you moving? It's really an amazing city.

SoftTalker 2026/03/27 11:16
芝加哥的一些地方还是不错的。最好避免大面积的接触。
Parts of Chicago are nice. Vast swathes of it are best avoided.
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/03/27 12:37
我个人的观点是最好避开整个旧金山，所以我对我的评估感到满意，即使规定， 争论，你刚才说的。 :)
My personal opinion is that all of San Francisco is best avoided, so I feel comfortable with my assessment even stipulating, arguendo, what you just said. :)
在 HN 中查看
vogtb 2026/03/27 12:00
我在芝加哥生活了大约十年，大约在 2017 年到 2023 年期间，我设定了一个目标：骑自行车去往 76 个（？）社区中的每一个社区。我已经到了 63 岁左右。肯定有比其他社区更粗糙的社区。但说实话，每个社区都有美味的食物，而且，你知道，住在那里的都是普通人。就像，在加菲猫公园，我记得从一个刚刚在卡车后面煮排骨的人那里买了排骨。他们太棒了。比尔森（Pilsen）有很棒的墨西哥食物，德文郡（Devon）有坚果印度食物（Ghareeb Narwaz 是迄今为止我最喜欢的）。是的，在一些社区，几个街区里都有人在街角卖东西。但没有人打扰任何人。周六下午 1 点，没有什么地方是你不能去的。 基本上，我想说的是，你在新闻中看到的芝加哥不是我住的地方。所以，你知道，保持警惕，小心，但我认为芝加哥的每个人都应该去一个随机的社区并买点食物。
I lived in Chicago for about a decade, and around 2017 to 2023 I set a goal of biking to every one of the 76(?) neighborhoods. I made it to about 63. There are definitely neighborhoods rougher than others. But tbh every neighborhood has good food, and, you know, normal people living there. Like, in Garfield park I remember buying ribs from a guy that was just sorta cooking them outa the back of his truck. They were terrific. Pilsen has great Mexican food, and Devon has nuts Indian food (Ghareeb Narwaz is by far my favorite). Yeah, in some neighborhoods, on a couple of blocks there are guys out on the corner selling. But no one's bothering anyone. There's nowhere you can't go on a Saturday at 1PM. Basically, what I'm saying is, the Chicago you see on the news isn't the one I live in. So, you know, keep your wits about you, take care, but I think everyone in Chicago should go to a random neighborhood and get some food.
在 HN 中查看
qingcharles 2026/03/27 13:11
我住过那里一些最简陋的社区，走来走去从来没有感到特别不安全。有几次人们把尸体扔在我的院子里，但当我住在一个非常好的地区时，我也遇到了这个问题:D 还有几次，当我家外面发生帮派枪战时，我去了地下室。

芝加哥有很多美味的食物。市中心对我很好。您可以骑自行车+El（地铁）游览整个城市。

尽管如此，它仍然是我最不喜欢的大城市。我没有再住在那里的冲动。

I've lived in some of the roughest neighborhoods there and never felt particularly unsafe walking around at all. A couple of times people dumped bodies in my yard, but I also had that problem when I lived in a really nice area too :D A couple of other times I took to my basement while there were gang shootouts happening outside my house.

There is lots of great food in Chicago. Downtown is nice to me. You can get around the whole city on bike + El (subway).

It's still my least favorite major city, though. I have no urge to live there again.

在 HN 中查看

Good luck. The Malört is as bad as advertised.

Nobody was surprised when Jeppsons got in on the hand sanitizer effort during COVID. Didn’t even have to change the recipe.

binarynate 2026/03/27 07:41

unpaywalled: https://archive.is/kkKUW

posters on artist's site: https://www.thechicagoneighborhoods.com/shop

cozzyd 2026/03/27 09:02

If only the Loop one were centered on Madison instead of Monroe so it would have my condo in it...
在 HN 中查看
reg_dunlop 2026/03/27 08:04
谢谢！刚买了4个！
Thanks! Just bought 4!
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/03/27 08:21
哪四个？ （我们有贝弗利，我长大的地方，还有加菲猫公园，只是因为它看起来很酷。）
Which four? (We have Beverly, where I grew up, and Garfield Park, just because it's cool looking.)
在 HN 中查看
reg_dunlop 2026/03/27 09:28
林肯公园、林肯广场（称这两个街区为家），然后是萨默和考德威尔百合池。许多个夏日的夜晚，我都在弗兰克·盖里乐队 (Frank Gehry Bandshell) 前的长廊下度过。还有荷花池……那些海报令人惊叹。确实唤起了一种地方感和对这座城市的热爱。
Lincoln Park, Lincoln Square (called those 2 neighborhoods home) then Summer and Caldwell Lily Pond. I spent many a summer evening under the promenade in front of the Frank Gehry Bandshell; And the Lily Pond...those posters are stunning. Really evoked a sense of place and love for the city.
在 HN 中查看
tptacek 2026/03/27 09:58
不错的选择。如果您还不知道，瑞安·杜根 (Ryan Duggan) 制作了一系列标志性的邻里披萨店印刷品。我曾两次把《贝弗利福克斯》挂在墙上，两次我都不得不把那张照片送给那些来到我家时被它一枪打中的人。

https://www.ryanduggan.com/

Good picks. If you didn't already know, Ryan Duggan did a print series of iconic neighborhood pizza places. I've had Fox's in Beverly on my wall twice and both times I ended up having to give the print away to someone who was one-shot by it when they came to my house.

https://www.ryanduggan.com/

在 HN 中查看
mikeaskew4 2026/03/27 10:21
无论是福克斯餐厅还是米兰餐厅，我不知道贝弗利/摩根公园/EP 的任何人怎么会每周至少吃两次披萨。
With both Fox’s and Milano’s, I don’t know how anyone in Beverly/Morgan Park/EP doesn’t have pizza at least twice a week.

wetpaws 2026/03/27 09:17 deleted
[死的]
[dead]
在 HN 中查看
claudiug 2026/03/27 08:55
可悲的是，你拿着这篇文章，去任何法学硕士，要求阅读并构建一个系统提示，从图像+文本，然后你可以复制他的工作
what is sad, is that you an take the article, go to any LLM, ask to read and build an system prompt, from the images + text, and then you can replicate his work
在 HN 中查看
reg_dunlop 2026/03/27 09:39
我认为更可悲的是有人不遗余力地不支持艺术家。

除其他外，艺术是对话的开端。如果有人看到法学硕士生成的东西挂在家里或办公室并开始对话，它会是这样的：“是的，我在网上看到它，最后我把它撕掉了”……或者这个人为了保全面子而撒谎（也很伤心）。

或者，对话可能是“是的，这些是直接从艺术家那里购买的……我买它们是因为……”。 （更有趣的对话）

这两者都会引起对话者的情绪反应。

I think what's more sad is someone going out of their way to NOT support the artist.

Art is, among other things, a conversation starter. If someone sees an LLM generated thing hanging in the home or office and strikes up a conversation, it goes something like: "Yeah, i saw it online and I ended up ripping it off"...or the person lies to save face (also sad).

Or, the conversation could be "Yeah, these were purchased directly from the artist...I bought them because....". (a much more interesting conversation)

Both of these evoke an emotional reaction with an interlocutor.

在 HN 中查看
mikeaskew4 2026/03/27 10:24
是的，而且看起来完全是垃圾。人们在山前的棕榈树下吃着纽约切片，穿着海豚队的球衣。
Yes, and it’d look like total crap. People eating NY slices, wearing Dolphins jerseys under palm trees in front of mountains.

Yeah, that would be a real scummy thing to do!
